mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, causing immediate negative pressure on S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 295 on the 1982-1984 base index, forced monetary tightening and eroded consumer purchasing power trigger a sustained drawdown in equity markets over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns are positive in >55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 295, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 after 2022.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and a decline in total nonfarm payrolls signals labor market deterioration that immediately reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects resolving supply chain bottlenecks and immediate earnings revisions, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in <50% of periods following INDPRO increases, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in housing starts signal weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction sector activity, which systematically precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines are followed by positive 20d returns in >65% of cases, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 3.5 percent, borrowing costs for corporations and consumers rise sufficiently to compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 60d returns in >58% of triggered instances, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance during rate hike cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained unemployment rates below 4.0 percent indicate robust labor demand and wage growth that support corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE below 4.0 percent coincides with negative 20d returns in >60% of cases, or the mechanism fails during inflationary periods where low unemployment triggers aggressive Fed tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in real GDP and retail sales confirms a broad-based demand shock that forces earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS is followed by positive 60d returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.
